mnemonics.title: Key Generation Steps
mnemonics.content: Prime Pair, Multiply Near, Totient Here, Exponent Clear, Secret Dear (1. Choose primes p & q 2. Compute n = p×q 3. Calculate φ(n) = (p-1)(q-1) 4. Select public exponent e 5. Find private exponent d)

mnemonics.title: Encryption/Decryption Rule
mnemonics.content: Message to Exponent, Modulus Prevent (C = M^e mod n for encryption, M = C^d mod n for decryption)

mnemonics.title: Euler's Theorem Connection
mnemonics.content: Euler's φ(n) is Key, (m^φ(n) ≡ 1 mod n) → Ensures decryption works when ed ≡ 1 mod φ(n)

mnemonics.title: Coprimality Check
mnemonics.content: e and φ(n) Must Be Friends (coprime) → Use GCD(e, φ(n)) = 1 to find valid public exponent

acronyms.expansion: Pick p & q, Calculate n, φ(n), Choose e, Compute d

acronyms.usage: Remember all calculations (n, e, d) involve modular arithmetic
Core components of the RSA process

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a padlock made by interlocking two prime-shaped puzzle pieces (p & q)
Picture a hidden gear mechanism behind n that only the key generator can see
Visualize e and d as interlocking gears where e×d ≡ 1 mod φ(n) makes them turn perfectly
Imagine numbers wrapping around a giant clock face with n hours

analogies.analogy: Like a padlock (public key) anyone can lock, but only the unique key (private key) can open
Like trying to find original ingredients from a blended smoothie - easy to mix, hard to separate
Like a 24-hour clock where 25 becomes 1 - numbers wrap around after reaching modulus n
